title: Memory Cgroup Limit Enforcement
dataSources: `sourcetype=syslog` (cgroup OOM and memory events) and/or `custom:cgroup_memory` with usage vs. limit from cgroup v1/v2
app: `Splunk_TA_nix` plus optional custom script for cgroup tree metrics
spl:
index=os sourcetype=syslog ("memory.max_usage_in_bytes" OR "Out of memory") "cgroup"
| stats count by host, cgroup_id
| where count > 0
description: Surfaces kernel and runtime messages that show cgroup memory pressure, including OOM-style messages tied to cgroups, so you can act before services are throttled or killed at the limit.
value: Right-sizing container memory, adjusting limits, or moving workloads is easier when you see which cgroup names hit the wall, instead of only seeing generic out-of-memory noise.
implementation: Forward `kern`/`containerd`/`kubelet`-class syslog that includes cgroup paths or IDs. Prefer structured `custom:cgroup_memory` events with `current`, `max`, and `cgroup_id` for ratio alerts; this search starts from raw syslog keywords and counts hits per host and cgroup id.
knownFalsePositives: Test clusters that intentionally trigger cgroup OOM; log-format changes that still contain the word cgroup but refer to a healthy accounting line; missing `cgroup_id` parsing so every row lands in nullтАФfix extractions first.

references.title: Splunk Add-on for Unix and Linux
Splunk Lantern тАФ use case library

detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
тАв Ingest the OS log stream that records cgroup memory events (often forwarded as syslog) or deploy a `custom:cgroup_memory` input that samples `/sys/fs/cgroup`.

Step 1 тАФ Configure data collection
For syslog, ensure the forwarder is allowed to read `/var/log/kern.log` or `journal` streams with kernel priority. For structured metrics, sample usage and limit in MiB and tag `cgroup_id` from the path.

Step 2 тАФ Create the search and alert

```spl
index=os sourcetype=syslog ("memory.max_usage_in_bytes" OR (("Out of memory" OR "oom") AND "cgroup"))
| stats count by host, cgroup_id
| where count > 0
```

Adjust keywords to match your distributionтАЩs wording. Add `rex` to extract `cgroup_id` if the field is only in `_raw`.

**Understanding this SPL** тАФ Counts qualifying messages per host and cgroup; raise when any appear in the window (tighten to `>5` in busy environments).


Step 3 тАФ Validate
Reproduce a benign limit in a test cgroup and compare host-side `systemd-cgtop` or `cat` of the cgroupтАЩs `memory.current` to the log lines Splunk received.

Step 4 тАФ Operationalize
Route to the platform or container SRE queue with links to the namespace or workload, and add a follow-up search on usage/limit ratio if you add structured metrics.
grandmaExplanation: We watch for signs that a container or service group is bumping into its memory cap, so you can add memory or move work before things get stopped unexpectedly.
